records.title: ESA New Norcia NNO-3 / DSA-4 commissioning-state asset record

structured_summary.access_class: institutional / cross-support; public GSaaS access not assumed

database_value: Separate physical commissioning, operational acceptance and commercial availability states.
Store antenna-level capability instead of inheriting all site capabilities to every asset.
Track future-service dates as lifecycle events rather than overwriting current status.

confidence.reason: Network and antenna details are consistent with ESA-linked public summaries, but current operational acceptance and exact service interfaces require direct ESA confirmation.

pending_confirmation: Whether NNO-3 entered routine service after March 2026
Exact transmit and receive frequency ranges
G/T, EIRP, tracking rates and polarization
Supported CCSDS SLE services and versions

sources.title: ESTRACK network and antenna summary
sources.source_type: public secondary summary with ESA references

records.title: ESTRACK owned, augmented and cooperative network responsibility model

network_layers: cooperative stations supplied through inter-agency agreements
structured_summary.operations_model: Mission connections and core network operations are coordinated through ESOC / the Network Operations Centre; physical assets may be owned and operated by different organizations.
structured_summary.commercial_implication: A station appearing in a network map does not establish ownership, booking authority, pricing authority or uniform SLA responsibility.

database_value: Model network membership independently from station ownership and station operation.
Represent service contracts and cooperation agreements as time-bounded relationships.
Attach SLA, licence, maintenance, security and booking responsibilities to the relevant party.

confidence.reason: The layered network structure is well established publicly; individual agreements, current station membership and responsibility allocation are not fully public.

pending_confirmation: Current augmented and cooperative station roster

sources.title: ESTRACK network composition
sources.source_type: public secondary summary with ESA references

records.title: In-orbit edge processing and space data-center impact on ground-segment demand

structured_summary.architecture: Software-driven, multi-tenant in-orbit processing platforms use onboard or orbital compute to transform raw payload data into reduced or actionable products before downlink.
ground_segment_effect: Potential reduction in raw downlink volume
Shift from bulk raw-data delivery to product/event delivery
More variable contact demand based on processing completion and priority
Possible use of inter-satellite links before selecting a downlink gateway
Need to schedule compute, storage, ISL and ground contacts jointly
structured_summary.applicability: Architecture and capacity-planning model; not evidence of a generally available commercial GSaaS service.

database_value: Store generated raw volume separately from required downlink volume.
Model processing location and compression/productization before contact scheduling.
Allow contact requests to depend on compute completion, product priority and delivery deadline.

confidence.reason: The architecture and capacity pressures are supported by a 2026 research paper, but economic forecasts and commercial implementation assumptions remain scenario-dependent.

pending_confirmation: Actual in-orbit compute throughput and power budget
Processing reliability and fallback to raw downlink
Ground API for compute-completion and downlink-trigger events

sources.title: Deep Tech to Space: Space Data Centers and AI Revolution at the Edge

safety_note: These records are research candidates. Planned, institutional, cooperative or research capabilities must not be treated as commercially bookable capacity without direct operator verification.
